title: CareerForge LinkedIn Integration
goal: Integrate LinkedIn as both an import source (resume auto-fill) and an optimization target (AI rewrite for headline/About/experience), positioning CareerForge AI against competitors like Resume.io and Enhancv.

phases.name: LinkedIn PDF Import + Optimizer
phases.goal: Build /linkedin page with full upload → review → optimize flow.
deliverables: src/app/api/linkedin/optimize/route.ts (ResumeData → optimized; reuses Python backend, with rule-based fallback)
src/components/linkedin/upload-step.tsx (drag-drop PDF, inline help for LinkedIn Data Export)
Navbar link added (LinkedinNavIcon SVG, since lucide-react v1.21 lacks Linkedin icon)

phases.name: LinkedIn OAuth (Sign in with LinkedIn)
items: Apply at developer.linkedin.com for OAuth access
Request scopes: openid profile email w_member_social
On callback: pull profile, optionally create user, store access token
Add 'Continue with LinkedIn' button to /login and /signup

phases.name: Apply via LinkedIn + Job Sync
items: Add 'Apply on LinkedIn' button to /jobs/[id] cards (deep link to linkedin.com/jobs/view/{external_id})
Use Adzuna or The Muse API for job search (avoids LinkedIn partnership $$)

architecture_notes.pdf_parsing: Uses existing /api/parse (with regex fallback) for PDF text extraction, then server-side /api/linkedin/import parses LinkedIn-specific structure. Avoids server-side pdfjs-dist complexity.
architecture_notes.ai_optimization: Reuses Python backend /api/optimize with LinkedIn-specific instructions. Falls back to rule-based rewrites when Python backend unavailable (so demo works offline).
architecture_notes.save_to_dashboard: POSTs to existing /api/resumes endpoint, creating a new Resume in Prisma.
architecture_notes.icon_strategy: lucide-react v1.21 doesn't export Linkedin icon. Used inline SVG paths (Linkedin logo) in upload-step.tsx, client.tsx, and navbar.tsx (as LinkedinNavIcon component).

next_session_checklist: Read this file (Phase 1 done, Phase 2-3 pending)
Test /linkedin: login → upload any PDF → review parsed data → optimize → save
Verify Python backend integration by running: cd python-backend && uvicorn main:app --reload
